skill.name: sonoscli
skill.summary: Control Sonos speakers on the local network via CLI commands.
skill.author: aiskillstore

security_audit.summary: This is an official skill from the Anthropic skills repository. The skill provides CLI-based control of Sonos speakers on the local network. The skill uses the sonos CLI binary which must be installed separately. Network access is required for speaker discovery and control. No security concerns identified.

content.user_title: Control Sonos speakers with natural language
content.value_statement: Managing Sonos speakers manually via app or CLI can be tedious. This skill enables Claude to control playback, volume, grouping, and more through simple commands.
actual_capabilities: Discover Sonos speakers on the local network using SSDP or direct IP
Check speaker status including current track and playback state
Control playback with play, pause, and stop commands
Adjust volume levels on individual speakers
Manage speaker groups (join, unjoin, party mode, solo)
Access and play favorites and queue management
limitations: Requires the sonos CLI binary to be installed on the system
Network access is required for speaker discovery and control
Spotify search requires optional SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables

use_cases.title: Smart home enthusiasts
use_cases.description: Integrate Sonos control into automated workflows and scripts for whole-home audio management.
use_cases.target_user: Smart home enthusiasts who want CLI-based audio control

use_cases.title: Home office users
use_cases.description: Quickly pause or adjust volume on Sonos speakers without switching to a phone app.
use_cases.target_user: Remote workers with Sonos systems in home offices

use_cases.title: Power users and developers
use_cases.description: Build custom automation scripts and workflows that interact with Sonos speakers programmatically.

prompt_templates.title: Discover speakers
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all Sonos speakers on my network
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to identify available speakers for control

prompt_templates.title: Check speaker status
prompt_templates.prompt: What is my Kitchen speaker currently playing?
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to know the current playback state of a speaker

prompt_templates.title: Control playback
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to control playback on a specific speaker

prompt_templates.title: Manage volume and groups
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn up the volume on my Office speaker and group it with the Kitchen speaker
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need to adjust volume and manage speaker groupings

output_examples.input: Check the status of my Living Room speaker
output_examples.output:
Living Room speaker: Playing
Track: Bohemian Rhapsody - Queen
Artist: Queen
Album: A Night at the Opera
Position: 1:24 / 5:56
Volume: 45

output_examples.input: Discover all speakers on the network
output_examples.output:
Found 3 Sonos speakers:
- Living Room (192.168.1.100)
- Kitchen (192.168.1.101)
- Bedroom (192.168.1.102)
Kitchen has been joined to Living Room. Both speakers are now playing the same audio.

best_practices: Always specify the speaker name with --name flag when controlling specific speakers
Use `sonos discover` first to verify speaker IP addresses if you encounter connectivity issues
Set up SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET for full search functionality
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to control non-Sonos speakers with sonos commands
Do not skip the --name flag when multiple speakers are on the network
Do not ignore network permission warnings on macOS; enable local network access for the relevant application

faq.question: How do I install the sonos CLI?
Why is sonos discover failing with no route to host?
faq.answer: On macOS, enable local network access for your terminal or IDE in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Local Network.

faq.question: Can I control volume with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, use sonos volume set <level> --name "Speaker Name" to adjust volume.

faq.question: How do I group multiple speakers together?
faq.answer: Use sonos group join --name "Speaker1" --with "Speaker2" to group speakers, or sonos group party for all speakers.

faq.question: What is required for Spotify search?
faq.answer: Set SPOTIFY_CLIENT_ID and SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET environment variables before using sonos smapi search.

faq.question: Can I specify a speaker by IP instead of name?
faq.answer: Yes, use --ip <speaker-ip> instead of --name if SSDP discovery is not working.
